name: Glory
primary_sources: Mayes, Lawrence (2003), Glories – an Atmospheric Phenomenon
Nussenzveig, H. Moysés (2012). 'The Science of the Glory'. Scientific American

visual_attributes.morphology: concentric rings around the shadow of the observer's head
distinctive_features: red on the outside, bluish towards the center
centered on the antisolar or antilunar point
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: rings are successively dimmer and rarely complete

domain_specific_visual.medium: tiny water droplets in mist or clouds

functional_attributes.primary_function: optical phenomenon caused by light interaction

domain_specific_functional.wave_interference: caused by wave interference of light

relationships.description: Often seen in association with the Brocken spectre, a magnified shadow of the observer.
Can occur simultaneously with a rainbow under the right conditions.

symbolic_items: Great Seal of the United States

contextual_attributes.origin: observed globally, notably in the Harz mountains and Mount Emei
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: global, often in mountainous or misty regions
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes personal enlightenment and divinity in various cultures
primary_sources: Mayes, Lawrence (2003), Glories – an Atmospheric Phenomenon

contrastive_constraints: NOT caused by large water droplets
